tool.name: notseq
tool.summary: Exclude one sequence record by 1-based ordinal position

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `notseq` removes exactly one selected sequence record from a local sequence stream and returns the remaining records in their original order. The tool reuses the shared EMBOSS-RS sequence loader and sequence-collection result path instead of embedding format-specific stream logic.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current v1 interface accepts one local sequence input path plus one exclusion index. The index is **1-based** and must refer to an existing record in the loaded input stream.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits the remaining sequence records through the shared FASTA output path. CLI output also includes the standard EMBOSS-RS method summary lines reporting the input path, excluded index, total input count, returned record count, and FASTA output format.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `emboss-rs notseq`. Validation currently covers interior exclusion, first-record exclusion, single-record all-excluded behavior, and malformed or out-of-range failure cases.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release supports exclusion by ordinal position only. Identifier-based exclusion, multiple exclusion criteria, and duplicate-identifier policies remain deferred. Excluding the only record is allowed and returns an empty output stream.

artifacts.label: Three-record FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed multi-record FASTA fixture used to validate deterministic exclusion order.

examples.title: Exclude the second record from a three-record FASTA input
examples.description: Removes the middle record from a small FASTA fixture and returns the remaining records in stable source order.

expected_outputs.label: Filtered sequence collection
expected_outputs.description: A FASTA sequence collection containing the first and third records from the source fixture.
